footer.about: Authentic Mexican cuisine made by Mexican hands in Miami. Hand-nixtamalized corn, Oaxacan chiles, and recipes that cross borders without losing their soul.

testimonials.title: Happy Diners
testimonials.subtitle: Discover why hundreds of diners enjoy our cuisine every day.
testimonials.heading: What Our Diners Say

1.name: Ana G.
1.role: Mexican, living in Miami for 8 years
1.text: The most authentic mole I've tried outside Puebla. I cried with happiness.

2.name: Michael T.
2.text: Finally a place in Miami that understands corn. The cochinita tacos are a religious experience.

3.name: Laura and Carlos
3.text: Every Sunday we come for enchiladas and a michelada. It reminds us of home.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about your visit to Cielo y Maíz.

items.question: Do you have vegetarian or vegan options?
items.answer: Yes, we have tlacoyos, vegetarian enchiladas (with mushrooms or zucchini), and we can adapt several dishes. Ask your server.

items.question: Do you offer delivery?
items.answer: Yes, through Uber Eats and DoorDash. You can also order directly via WhatsApp for pickup in 20 minutes.

items.question: Do you accept credit cards?
items.answer: Yes, all major cards, Apple Pay, and cash. We don't accept checks.

items.question: Do you have parking?
items.answer: There's street parking (free after 6pm) and a parking lot half a block away for $5 all day.

about.title: Our Story
about.subtitle: Where Mexican tradition meets Miami's warmth
about.story: Founded in 2019 by the Vega brothers, originally from Puebla and living in Miami for over 15 years. They started as a small stand at a local fair; today they are a gathering point for the Mexican community and food lovers. Every recipe comes from their grandmother Doña Elena.
about.story_extra: Every morning, before we open our doors, our team hand-nixtamalizes the corn following the ancestral method. The blue tortillas we serve carry the authentic flavor of Mexico, prepared with the same love that Doña Elena used in her kitchen in Puebla.

values.title: Our Values
values.authenticity_desc: Traditional recipes from Puebla, authentic ingredients from Mexico.
values.family_desc: Every dish carries the love of Grandma Doña Elena.
values.quality_desc: Hand-nixtamalized corn every morning, Oaxacan chiles.
values.community_desc: A gathering point in Little Havana.

team.title: The Vega Brothers
team.description: Bringing the flavor of Puebla to Miami, one dish at a time.

cta.title: Come Meet Us
cta.subtitle: Discover the authentic taste of Mexico in Miami.

gallery.title: Our Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Discover our authentic Mexican dishes prepared with tradition and love.

tlacoyos.name: Black Bean and Nopal Tlacoyos
tlacoyos.description: Blue corn masa filled with black beans, grilled nopal, fresh cheese, cream, and salsa verde.

ceviche.name: Octopus Ceviche 'Acapulco'
ceviche.description: Tender octopus in lime juice with serrano chili, cilantro, red onion, and avocado.

esquites.name: Street-Style Esquites
esquites.description: Corn kernels with mayo, cotija cheese, chili powder, lime, and a hint of epazote.

quesadillas.name: Rajas and Cheese Quesadillas
quesadillas.description: Fresh tortillas filled with roasted poblano strips, melted Oaxaca cheese, epazote, and cream.

cochinita.name: Immigrant Cochinita Tacos
cochinita.description: Achiote pork slow-cooked for 12 hours with sour orange, blue tortillas, pickled onions, and habanero.

mole.name: Traditional Mole Poblano
mole.description: Made with 28 ingredients and 6 hours of cooking. Chicken breast with dark mole, red rice, and beans.

enchiladas.name: Enchiladas de Olla
enchiladas.description: Guajillo-sauce tortillas stuffed with Oaxaca cheese or beef tinga, topped with cream, onion, and fresh cheese.

pescado.name: 'Miami Heat' Grilled Fish
pescado.description: Red snapper fillet marinated with ancho and guajillo chiles, wood-grilled, served with sweet potato and beans.

tres-leches.name: 'Miami Nights' Tres Leches Cake
tres-leches.description: Soft sponge soaked in three milks, topped with coconut meringue and lime zest.

churros.name: Cajeta-Filled Churros
churros.description: Crispy churros filled with goat-milk cajeta, dusted with cinnamon, served with Mexican chocolate.

mamey-sorbet.name: Mamey Sorbet with Chili and Lime
mamey-sorbet.description: Refreshing mamey sorbet with a touch of chile piquin and key lime.

flan.name: Mexican Cajeta Flan
flan.description: Classic creamy flan with homemade caramel and cajeta, inspired by Grandma Dona Elena.
